changes.description: Updater waits for reachable release assets.
changes.details: The update check no longer uses a fixed first-seen delay. It now only hides or blocks a new update when the generated installer URL is not reachable yet, avoiding broken 404 installs without forcing a full 10-minute wait.

changes.description: Legacy Roaming files are cleaned up.
changes.details: Discord auth state is migrated from the old Roaming app folder into the LocalAppData LOA Tracker folder, and obsolete update delay state plus legacy party plan cache files are removed so the Roaming folder is left for window state only.

changes.description: LOA Logs monitoring no longer enables autostart.
changes.details: The Monitor LOA Logs Startup option now only watches while LOA Tracker is running and no longer registers a Windows startup entry unless Start with Windows or Start with Lost Ark is enabled.

changes.description: App can start hidden in the tray.
changes.details: General settings now includes a Hide on Launch option that starts LOA Tracker hidden in the tray area, including Windows startup launches when Start with Windows is enabled.
The release workflow now builds GitHub release notes from changelogs.json, so latest.json can stay untouched until the signed updater metadata is generated after release assets exist.
General system settings now use denser section spacing, smaller cards, and more compact controls so more options fit on screen without changing the clean settings structure.

changes.description: Updates tab labels roadmap items clearly.
changes.details: The known issues area is now labeled Coming Changes & Known Issues so low-priority roadmap ideas and active issues can live in the same section without confusing users.

changes.description: Updates tab uses space more efficiently.
changes.details: The updates view now uses a tighter layout with changelogs on the left and separate Known Issues and Coming Features panels stacked on the right.

changes.description: Known issues use compact detail rows.
changes.details: Known Issues and Coming Features now show short row descriptions with Details buttons for longer explanations, matching the changelog interaction pattern.

changes.description: Updates tab is optimized for smaller windows.
changes.details: Updates, changelogs, known issues, and coming features now use tighter typography, smaller labels, and a cleaner responsive layout for quarter-width and half-height window sizes.

changes.description: Update labels use clearer color groups.
changes.details: Changelog labels now use softer colors, known issues use severity labels from Critical downward, and coming features use separate priority labels such as No Prio, Low Prio, Mid Prio, and High Prio.

changes.description: Roadmap data is easier to maintain.
changes.details: Known issues and coming features are now split into separate resource sections, with additional roadmap items and clearer priorities for future work.
